verdicts.reason: Helaena's death (line 31) → murder rumor spreads (line 31) → riot erupts 'That night' (line 41). Immediate, same-night spark with no decisional mediator. TRIGGERS is correct.
Massacre at Cobbler's Square (lines 79-80) is a beat within the same riot-night event. SUB_BEAT_OF correctly reflects structural containment.
Sack of Celtigar manse (lines 49-51) is a concurrent beat within the riot's geography. SUB_BEAT_OF correctly captures simultaneity and structural nesting.
Riot breakdown of authority → whores' opportunistic free choice to crown Gaemon (lines 85-86). ENABLES correctly preserves agency; the riot is precondition, not cause.

Night-one's riot amplifies crowd to 'twice as large and thrice as fearful' (lines 115-116), priming for night-two march. ENABLES correctly captures amplification without direct causation; tier-2 flagged for Eustace attribution.
Shepherd's sermon (line 119) directly incites crowd ('Kill them! Kill them!' line 123) and march begins immediately. TRIGGERS is correct for direct spark.
Loss of pit dragons + Joffrey's death → counselors persuade flight (lines 245-247: 'King's Landing was lost, all agreed'). MOTIVATES correctly routes via human decision rather than forced causation.
Joffrey's fall from Syrax mid-riot (lines 161-163) → queen's frantic command sends seven knights into city (line 143). Same-night, immediate response. TRIGGERS is correct.

Rhaenyra's flight leaves castle 'half a day' before Perkin arrives demanding yield (lines 249-250). Her departure is precondition (removes best defenders) but Garth's choice to yield is his own decision. ENABLES is correct.

Garth yields castle, then Perkin executes him (line 251-252: 'Garth the Harelip was dragged before him and beheaded'). Perkin's active choice to show 'no mercy' mediates the outcome. MOTIVATES is correct.
Red Keep yields → Trystane free to mount throne next day (lines 259-260). Control of castle is precondition; Perkin's choice to install Trystane is free. ENABLES is correct.

Attack on Rhaenyra's camp during flight (lines 155-156: Byrch felled, Bentley mortally wounded). Same journey-event narrated within flight-to-dragonstone node. SUB_BEAT_OF correctly reflects narrative containment.

Rhaenyra's decision to head specifically for Dragonstone to find dragon eggs (lines 175-176 in p02 section) is her own choice that puts her in position for the ambush. ENABLES correctly preserves her agency.
Aegon II's prior seizure of Dragonstone (when Rhaenyra lands) sets the trap (line 197-198). Precondition for ambush but Aegon still chooses to feed her to Sunfyre. ENABLES is correct.
Fall of Dragonstone → king's immediate command to hang Gerardys (lines 279-280 in FAB-19). Same coup-night, direct royal order with no separate deliberation. TRIGGERS is correct.

Rhaenyra's death by dragon witnessed by Aegon III → formative trauma shaping his character and dragon-aversion (FAB-19 line 239). MOTIVATES targeting a character's development is per VERIFY-RULES guidance.
Gyldayn explicitly names the Kingsroad clash 'the last battle of the Dance of the Dragons' (FAB-19 line 215). Direct textual statement. PART_OF is correct structural containment.
Gyldayn states Tumbleton is 'only at Tumbleton did the dragons ever truly dance' (FAB-18-p02 line 101). Named in text as the truest 'dance' defining the war. PART_OF is correct.

Seasmoke landing at Riverrun persuades reluctant Elmo Tully to call banners (FAB-18-p02 lines 63-64). Troop strength is contributing precondition among many (discipline lax, leaders divided). ENABLES correctly avoids overstating singular cause.

Hard Hugh's coronation + horseshoe-nailing arrogance → lords summon secret council to plot his death (FAB-18-p02 lines 300-302). Deliberate decision by named lords mediates outrage and plot. MOTIVATES is correct.

Caltrops council resolution to kill Hammer → Jon Roxton executes him at Tumbleton chaos ('You died in the battle' line 317). Plot goal realized via intermediate battle-cover. CAUSES is correct for realized conspiracy.

Seasmoke's surprise attack + burning stables → Hammer rushes half-dressed into yard where Roxton waits (lines 330-332). Battle chaos enables assassination opportunity. ENABLES correctly distinguishes plot (H-E21) from enabling circumstance.
Caltrops council goal to kill both dragonriders → Hobert poisons Ulf White (line 345-352). Continuation of same conspiracy's stated objectives through intermediate step. CAUSES is correct.

Accord guarantees Borros restores Aegon II to throne; later Velaryon submission removes the sea obstacle (FAB-19 lines 49-89). Accord is political precondition enabling (not forcing) Aegon's return. ENABLES is correct.

Accord discussion of Velaryon problem and agreement to pursue Corlys negotiation (FAB-19 line 53-55). Opens door to separate negotiation. ENABLES correctly separates accord-promise from actual submission-negotiation.
Accord empowers Borros with city authority; next day he rides against Gaemon Palehair (line 71). Restored authority is enabling precondition for his action. ENABLES is correct.

Gaemon's mock kingdom atop Visenya's Hill existing is the target Borros crushes (line 71). Gaemon's court's existence is precondition (alongside accord H-E26). ENABLES for a contributing, non-sole precondition is correct.

Corlys's pardon and submission deliver twelve war galleys, removing the Velaryon sea obstacle for Aegon's crossing (FAB-19 lines 89-91). Velaryon power was standing blocker; submission removes it. ENABLES is correct (Aegon still chooses to sail).
Aegon returns and immediately judges 'dayfly kings' (line 97); Trystane sentenced to death as first target. Judicial decision by actor exercises authority. MOTIVATES is correct.
Restored king 'determined to exact vengeance' (line 119), urged on by Alicent. Explicit, mother-urged decision by actor. MOTIVATES is correct.

Vengeance campaign hardens rebel hearts and feeds mobilization ('only served to harden the hearts' line 123). Real causation through intermediate steps of hardening and gathering. CAUSES correctly reflects multi-step chain.
News of Baratheon's kingsroad defeat triggers coup execution 'the moment they learned' (line 249). Immediate trigger for planned coup. TRIGGERS is correct.
Same coordinated coup night as H-E32; Tom Tangletongue beheaded and Tom Tanglebeard drowned 'in that same hour' (lines 255-257). Parallel immediate consequence of same trigger. TRIGGERS is correct.
Queen Alicent arrested 'on the serpentine steps' same coup night (line 263). Part of coordinated sequence triggered by kingsroad news. TRIGGERS is correct.

Edge type should be TRIGGERS, not MOTIVATES. Text says the kingsroad defeat 'put their plans in motion' (line 249) — the defeat is the trigger for execution of pre-existing conspiracy, not the source of motivation for a decision. MOTIVATES requires target to be a decision or deciding character per VERIFY-RULES, not a death event. The death is the consequence of the triggered plan + execution.

Text grounds a naming decision, not a birth event. Line 293 states: 'upon learning of her lord's death in battle, Lady Baratheon named the child Royce, after her own father.' The battle motivates Lady Elenda's naming choice (born 7 days later regardless), not the biological birth itself. Birth-of-royce-baratheon conflates two distinct events — the birth and the naming. If the target node is the naming decision, edge type should be confirmed; if it's the biological birth, edge must be rejected or redirected to a naming-specific event.

Type should be SUSPECTED_OF, not AGENT_IN. Text hedges agency: 'whilst the hand that poisoned the Arbor red will never be known, we can have no doubt that it was done at the behest of Larys Strong' (FAB-19 line 277). The actual poisoner is unknown; Gyldayn asserts Larys ordered it with confidence, but it remains Gyldayn's interpretation. AGENT_IN implies direct participation in the poisoning role; Larys is the orchestrator, not the hands-on agent. Tier-2 + disputed flags are appropriate, but SUSPECTED_OF is the honest type when the actual agent is unknown and attribution is interpretive.

Aegon's death is precondition for bloodless handover (FAB-20 line 572: 'dead, long live the king'). Armies converging regardless, so death enables the peaceful character of arrival rather than forcing it. ENABLES is correct.
Aegon III's minority (age 10) is procedural/customary necessity binding him to regency (FAB-20 line 207). No decisional gap — age alone forces structure. TRIGGERS is correct for procedural causation.

Political stabilization across Hour of the Wolf (Cregan's judgments settling claims) is precondition for planned coronation/wedding to proceed; war could have derailed it (FAB-20 line 601-602). ENABLES correctly reflects that the peace enables rather than forces the wedding.

new_node_checks.reason: Era (dance-of-dragons), year (130 AC), and type (event.death) are consistent with canon. Identity accurately reflects her suicide from Maegor's Holdfast. Genuinely missing from prior spine — important trigger event for the riot.

Era (dance-of-dragons), year (130 AC), and type (event.council) are correct. Identity accurately summarizes the pact's terms (pardons, betrothal, restoration plan). Structurally necessary bridge event between post-Tumbleton maneuvering and Aegon II's return.

summary.flags_resolved: Orchestrator's pre-flagged three edges all confirmed as problematic: H-E36 rejected per naming/birth distinction, H-E37 adjusted to SUSPECTED_OF per unknown poisoner, H-E35 adjusted to TRIGGERS per pre-existing plan execution model.
